TPM 2.0 became a mandatory requirement for Windows 11 in 2021 -- vindicating the long-term thesis that hardware security would reach consumers
Aberdeen predicts business adoption of LT will drive some consumer adoption through worker crossover
Intel embedded firmware TPM 2.0 (Platform Trust Technology / PTT) in all Skylake and later consumer processors from 2015 onward -- bringing hardware root of trust to non-vPro consumer PCs

Windows 11 enabled Virtualization-Based Security (VBS) and Hypervisor-Protected Code Integrity (HVCI) by default on consumer hardware -- the functional equivalent of the trusted execution environment Kastner advocated in 2003. Microsoft's stated goal: give all consumer PCs the same enterprise-grade security as corporate machines.

Kastner's 2003 thesis that consumer PCs would require hardware-rooted trusted execution for identity protection and online trust was vindicated by the Windows 11 TPM 2.0 mandate and VBS/HVCI defaults affecting billions of devices -- even though the specific LaGrande brand was not the vehicle. Prescience upgraded from medium (6) to high (8).
